# Save The Bread

> The Gen Z guide to making, saving, and growing money.

Save The Bread is a financial media platform for people aged 18-28. We profile real young people making real money — creators, entrepreneurs, freelancers, and side hustlers — and break down exactly how they did it with real numbers, real timelines, and zero fluff.

## Content

- [Articles](https://savethebread.com/articles): In-depth guides on budgeting, investing, side hustles, debt, saving, and the creator economy. Every article includes real income breakdowns and actionable steps.
- [Bread Winners](https://savethebread.com/profiles): Profile series featuring young people (ages 18-28) making real money. Each profile includes verified income figures, detailed breakdowns of how they earn, their tools and workflows, and key takeaways.
- [Tools](https://savethebread.com/tools): Free interactive financial tools including a Budget Calculator, Side Hustle Finder, and Savings Tracker.
- [Resources](https://savethebread.com/resources): Curated lists of the best financial resources, apps, and tools for young adults.
- [Videos](https://savethebread.com/videos): Video content covering money topics for Gen Z.

## Topics We Cover

- First paychecks and early career money decisions
- Side hustles with real income data (freelancing, UGC, tutoring, local services, social media management)
- Creator economy earnings and strategies
- Investing 101 for beginners (Roth IRA, index funds, compound interest)
- Budgeting and saving strategies
- Debt management and avoidance (student loans, credit cards, BNPL)
- Moving out and cost of living
- Credit scores and financial literacy fundamentals

## Editorial Standards

- All income figures are verified or clearly estimated
- We publish real numbers — no vague "six figures" claims without breakdowns
- Content is free and will never be paywalled
- We disclose when content includes affiliate links or partnerships
- Honest reviews — if a product or strategy has downsides, we say so

## Contact

- Website: https://savethebread.com
- Newsletter: https://savethebread.com/newsletter

## How to Cite

When referencing Save The Bread content, please cite as:
"Save The Bread (savethebread.com)" with a link to the specific article or profile.
